org_readiness_checklist.soql: SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM Case WHERE CreatedDate = LAST_N_DAYS:90
SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM CaseHistory WHERE Field='Owner' AND CreatedDate = LAST_N_DAYS:90
SELECT COUNT() FROM FlowVersionView WHERE Status='Active' AND ProcessType='AutoLaunchedFlow' LIMIT 200  [Tooling API]
SELECT COUNT(Id) FROM ProcessInstance WHERE Status='Pending'
SELECT DeveloperName FROM NamedCredential LIMIT 20  [Tooling API]

calibration_gate.gate_rule: 3 of top 5 overlap AND zero FPs in algo top 5 (FPs outside top 5 are logged, not blocking). Direction: abs(impact_delta) <= 3 AND abs(effort_delta) <= 3.
calibration_gate.ranking_function: tier_order (Quick Win=1, Strategic=2, Complex=3), then (impact - effort) desc, then effort asc

impact_bias_note: Systematic low impact bias detected (avg=3.0). All opportunities score ≤ 4. Review _volume_pts() bands — the current fixture org has ~300 cases/90d = ~23/week which maps to volume_pts=2.0 (the floor). Consider lowering the volume_pts threshold bands so that 20-50 weekly records scores 4-5 pts rather than 2.0. Also review _W_VOLUME weight (currently 0.30) — if volume dominates and most orgs have moderate volume, the resulting impact will always cluster low.
borderline_thresholds: HANDOFF_FRICTION: proxy_ratio=1.046 — borderline fire. Validate this is a genuine signal in the live org before SF-3.2 calibration session. May need threshold +10%.

recommendations.observation: Systematic low impact bias detected (avg=3.0). All opportunities score ≤ 4. Review _volume_pts() bands — the current fixture org has ~300 cases/90d = ~23/week which maps to volume_pts=2.0 (the floor). Consider lowering the volume_pts threshold bands so that 20-50 weekly records scores 4-5 pts rather than 2.0. Also review _W_VOLUME weight (currently 0.30) — if volume dominates and most orgs have moderate volume, the resulting impact will always cluster low.
recommendations.rationale: Lowering volume thresholds means a dev org with 20-30 cases/week scores 3.0 pts instead of 2.0. Most enterprise Salesforce orgs have 100-500 cases/week — these would score 5.5-7.5. This brings expected impact into the 4-7 range for typical orgs. OVERFIT GUARD: after adjusting, simulate a mid-size org (200 cases/week) through the fixture — confirm it scores 5-7 impact, not 9-10. Bands calibrated only for a dev org will inflate scores on real customer orgs and destroy credibility.

recommendations.observation: HANDOFF_FRICTION: proxy_ratio=1.046 — borderline fire. Validate this is a genuine signal in the live org before SF-3.2 calibration session. May need threshold +10%.
recommendations.suggested_after: validate live org first; raise by 10% only if architect confirms not real
recommendations.rationale: Borderline fires should be validated before adjusting.

recommendations.observation: PM-05 (Named Credential → Flow scan) may be empty even if Named Credentials exist. Flow metadata shape differs by Flow type and packaging. Treat D5 as 'may not fire in all orgs' until the exact metadata path(s) are confirmed in the target org. Do not adjust D5 threshold based on a single org observation.
recommendations.suggested_after: no change — document observed metadata path for SF-4.3
recommendations.rationale: SF-1.2 flagged this risk. Confirmed as known variance point.

summary: SF-3.2 analysis-only mode (no architect assessment provided). 3 recommendation(s) generated from score_debug analysis. Provide architect_assessment.json to run the full calibration gate.
